source.title: Internally generated reactivation of single neurons in human hippocampus during free recall

confidence_rationale: Direct human single-unit evidence that an internally generated cue reinstates the original episode-specific trace, and does so ~1.5 s before the content reaches verbal report. This is the strongest available warrant that an internal goal/context vector is a legitimate retrieval cue (MECH-293's internally triggered probe). Held below 0.85 because it is correlational -- it shows reinstatement precedes report, not that the internal cue causes selection of that trace -- and it does not adjudicate the matching rule.
failure_signatures: Only a sparse, item-specific subset of episode-selective neurons reliably reactivated at recall -- not every stored trace is addressable by the internal cue.
The paper characterises successful reinstatement only; the common recall-omission case (internal cue evokes no reinstatement) is left uncharacterised, so the silent-miss boundary is unknown.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Human MTL neurons that fire selectively for a specific encoded episode reactivate during free recall of that episode, with reactivation preceding verbal report by ~1.5 s; retrieval is reinstatement of the encoding-time representation triggered by an internally generated (un-cued) signal.
mapping.ree_translation: Validates that an internally generated goal/context vector -- not only an externally presented stimulus -- is a legitimate retrieval cue that reinstates a stored trace. This is the biological warrant for MECH-293's awake ghost-probe (the agent re-probes deferred goals from an internal state), for MECH-292's ranked-retrieval premise, and for ARC-060's discrete addressable bank.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Correlational and from epileptic tissue. It establishes that internal-cue reinstatement happens and temporally leads report, but it does not show the internal cue causes selection of that particular trace, and it cannot adjudicate cosine-similarity vs attractor-completion as the matching function the bank should use.
mapping.source_context: Epilepsy patients with implanted hippocampal/entorhinal depth electrodes; audiovisual film-clip encoding followed by free verbal recall; single-unit recording.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (rare human intracranial single-unit, Science). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: internally triggered reinstatement is exactly MECH-293's premise, but episodic-memory recall is not goal re-probing. Transfer risk moderate: clinical tissue + correlational, and the matching rule is unspecified.
